summary: Iranian forces have deployed a modified "Ketral" strike/swarming drone with an X-shaped tail fin and rear rocket booster, now classified as a small loitering munition. Ukrainian units struck Russian positions in Kursk, destroying multiple FPV launch sites, depots, and shelters, while Russian forces used LMUR missiles to hit Ukrainian UAV launch points in Kharkiv. Iranian-backed militias conducted fiber-optic FPV drone attacks on U.S. facilities in Iraq, likely adopting tactics from Russian forces. Ukrainian operators are countering Russian drone use of log-periodic antennas on "Molniyas," which offer wider range and jamming resistance. Russian "Yolka" and "Elka" anti-drone systems continue kinetic interceptions of Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with operators maintaining clear communication under pressure.
